summary: Comprehensive backend testing of AI integration layer using Claude API. All 10 tests passed. Verified GET /api/ai/status, POST /api/ai/minutes-draft (Claude Sonnet), and POST /api/ai/governance-suggestions (Claude Haiku) endpoints are fully functional.
passed_tests: GET /api/ai/status returns ai_enabled=true with features and models
POST /api/ai/minutes-draft generates formal minutes with WHEREAS/RESOLVED structure
POST /api/ai/governance-suggestions returns 2-4 actionable suggestions with routes and point estimates
POST /api/ai/governance-suggestions works with optional trust_id parameter
POST /api/ai/minutes-draft works with minimal valid input
critical_code_review_comments: Claude integration uses emergentintegrations library with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY - working correctly
Minutes drafting uses Claude Sonnet (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929) for complex reasoning and formal document generation
Governance suggestions uses Claude Haiku (claude-haiku-4-5) for quick, efficient suggestions
Both endpoints properly authenticate users via get_current_user/require_write_access dependencies
Response models (MinutesDraftResponse, GovernanceSuggestionsResponse) properly validate AI outputs
JSON parsing includes fallback handling if Claude returns markdown-wrapped JSON
context_for_next_testing_agent: AI integration layer fully tested and working. GET /api/ai/status returns ai_enabled=true with features.minutes_drafting=true, features.governance_suggestions=true, models.drafting='claude-sonnet-4-5', models.suggestions='claude-3-5-haiku'. POST /api/ai/minutes-draft generates formal minutes with WHEREAS/RESOLVED structure including draft_body, suggested_title, and cautions. POST /api/ai/governance-suggestions returns 2-4 suggestions with title, description, route (/minutes/new, /calendar, etc.), and estimated_points_gain. Both endpoints require authentication and use real Claude API (no mocking).

features: Validates required fields (minutes_type, meeting_date, participants, decisions_outline, trust_name)
